name: Loanmax Title Loans
description_short: Loanmax Title Loans is located at 250 S Willow St, Manchester, NH 03103. Google reviewers rate the branch 5.0 stars across 485 reviews. Call +1 603-625-9048.
description_long:
LoanMax Title Loans was founded in 1990 in Jonesboro, Georgia — originally as "Instant Cash Loans on Car Titles, Inc." — and has since grown into one of the larger auto title lending chains in the United States. Headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia and led by founder and president Rod Aycox, the company operates approximately 176–194 stores across roughly 18–20 states including Florida, Georgia, Iowa, Idaho, Kansas, Nevada, Utah, Virginia, Delaware, Michigan, and South Carolina. LoanMax holds no CDFI certification, HUD approval, NFCC membership, or BBB accreditation — and carries an F rating from the Better Business Bureau, attributed to a documented pattern of failing to respond to consumer complaints.

LoanMax offers consumer auto title loans ranging from $100 to $10,000, secured by the customer's vehicle title. Borrowers retain possession of and continue driving their vehicle while the loan is active. Repayment structures include a standard 30-day single-installment option or multi-installment monthly plans. No credit check is required — eligibility is based primarily on vehicle value and ownership. The company prominently advertises cash in 20 minutes or less. APRs are triple-digit; in South Carolina, LoanMax publicly discloses rates as high as 372%. If a borrower cannot repay on time, loan rollovers are permitted up to six times, with interest required at each rollover. Origination fees and full fee schedules are not publicly itemized on the company website.

LoanMax's primary differentiators are speed, accessibility, and minimal eligibility barriers. Approval decisions require no credit pull, making the product available to consumers locked out of traditional lending channels. The company funds loans in under 20 minutes, and its broad store network provides in-person service in communities often underserved by banks and credit unions. Owner Rod Aycox has also directed charitable giving through his personal foundation, funding projects including the MLK Memorial, toy drives, and programs serving homeless populations — though this has no direct bearing on the loan product or its terms.

Honestly assessed, LoanMax is a high-cost lender of last resort with meaningful consumer risk. The BBB's F rating — driven by approximately 200 complaints over three years and the company's consistent failure to respond — is a concrete warning sign. Independent review platforms reinforce this picture: PissedConsumer averages 2.1 out of 5 stars across 41 reviews, with recurring complaints about aggressive repossession practices, difficulty obtaining title releases after payoff, and fee disputes. The triple-digit APR structure makes these loans extremely expensive to carry or roll over, and pledging a vehicle as collateral introduces the real possibility of losing transportation. LoanMax may resolve an immediate cash crisis for a vehicle owner with no other qualifying options, but the repossession risk, cost of borrowing, and complaint record make it a choice of last resort rather than a recommended financial product.

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee offered. Auto title loans are not subscription or service products; all loan terms are set at origination.

pros: No credit check required — loan approval based on vehicle value, not credit history
Cash disbursed in 20 minutes or less at in-store locations
Customer keeps and drives their vehicle throughout the entire loan term
Loan amounts from $100 to $10,000 accommodate a range of emergency cash needs
Up to 6 loan rollovers available for borrowers who need additional repayment time
176+ store locations across approximately 18–20 states for in-person service
5.0/5 Google rating at the Manchester, NH location based on 478 reviews
cons: BBB F rating with approximately 200 complaints in 3 years; company cited for failure to respond to consumer complaints
APR publicly disclosed as high as 372% in South Carolina — among the most expensive consumer loan products available
Rollovers extend the debt cycle at high cost — interest must be paid at each of the up to 6 permitted rollovers
Vehicle repossession is a direct consequence of default — borrowers put their primary transportation at risk
PissedConsumer average of 2.1/5 stars across 41 reviews, with documented complaints about repossession tactics and delays in releasing paid-off titles
best_for: Vehicle owners with poor or no credit who need same-day emergency cash and own their car outright
Borrowers who have been declined by banks or personal loan lenders and have a vehicle with equity to pledge
Consumers facing urgent short-term cash shortfalls between $100 and $10,000 with no qualifying alternatives
Individuals in LoanMax's operating states who cannot access credit unions, CDFIs, or lower-cost emergency loan products
diagnosis: LoanMax Title Loans is a last-resort option for vehicle owners with poor credit who need emergency cash immediately and have exhausted lower-cost alternatives. The core risks are substantial: APRs publicly exceeding 372%, an F BBB rating driven by repeated failure to address consumer complaints, and documented problems with repossession and title release mean borrowers should seriously consider credit unions, CDFIs, or employer advance programs before pledging their vehicle here.
services: In-store loan origination and same-day cash funding
No-credit-check loan approval based on vehicle value
Cash disbursement in 20 minutes or less
Loan rollovers and extensions (up to 6 times)
In-person loan servicing at 176+ retail store locations
